title: Steven Fenyves, CFP, CFS - Valued Wealth Management Inc.

details:
**Succession:** 21 years in practice | Solo practice with small team | Named after service ("Valued Wealth")
**AUM:** $50M-150M** (based on solo practice with 2 employees, 21 yea
**Why:** Solo founder (no partnership complexity) | 21 years = mature practice, peak value | Independent BD affiliation (Osaic) = portable book

**Subject:** "21 years in Boca—what's your succession plan?"

Steven,

I came across Valued Wealth Management and saw you founded the practice in 2005. That's 21 years serving Boca Raton professionals—impressive longevity in a competitive market.

I'm reaching out because I work with advisors in similar positions: solo practice owners who've built something valuable over 20+ years and are now thinking about what the next 5-10 years look like.

details:

title: Joshua Feller, ChFC
name: Joshua Feller, ChFC
details:
**Succession:** 20 years in practice | Partnership structure | "Old-school relationship-focused"
**AUM:** $50M-200M** (partnership, 20 years, Boca Raton market)
**Why:** 20-year milestone = natural evaluation point | Partnership without clear succession = both partne | Relationship-focused practice = high client retent
approach:
Opener:

**Subject:** "20 years as partners—succession planning?"

Josh and Richard,

I noticed you co-founded Feller Financial in 2006—20 years ago. That's impressive, especially as partners (most partnerships don't last 20 years!).

I'm reaching out because I work with advisor partnerships in similar positions. The 20-year mark often raises the succession question: who exits first? How do you value each partner's book? What if one wants to retire and the other wants to keep working?
